mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is compatible with the Kafka API, designed for developers who need a lightweight and fast solution without the complexities of ZooKeeper or JVM. It is ideal for real-time data processing and analytics, offering a simplified operational experience. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes for orchestration and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Redpanda CLI tool, which is necessary for managing Redpanda clusters.

```bash

curl -1sLf 'https://dl.redpanda.com/nzc4ZYQK3WRGd9sy/redpanda/cfg/setup/bash.deb.sh' | sudo -E bash

curl -1sLf 'https://dl.redpanda.com/nzc4ZYQK3WRGd9sy/redpanda/cfg/setup/bash.rpm.sh' | sudo -E bash

```

> For Debian/Ubuntu systems.
> For Fedora/RedHat/Amazon Linux systems.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create redpanda-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure gcloud CLI is installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to access the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials redpanda-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials redpanda-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Redpanda on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step installs Redpanda using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --version 25.3.10 --namespace redpanda --create-namespace

```

> Add the Redpanda Helm repository.
> Install Redpanda in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Redpanda deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/main/examples/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/main/examples/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring for Redpanda.
> Set up autoscaling for Redpanda.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Redpanda deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

> This removes the Redpanda release.
> This deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process involves draining nodes, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --version 25.3.10 --namespace redpanda

kubectl get pods -n redpanda

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> Upgrade Redpanda to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running and in a 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Redpanda service is not running or the network is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Redpanda pods with 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Scale up your Kubernetes cluster or reduce the resource requests in your Redpanda deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Redpanda pod is failing to start due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redpanda' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Redpanda Helm release has not been installed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Redpanda using the Helm install command provided in the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for high-throughput workloads, added support for tiered storage, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required when upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in the 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda'.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials redpanda-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and the necessary tools installed.
